consensus.pattern: run = create+start collapsed into one foreground ephemeral one-shot; persistence is an explicit opt-in, not a separate verb
consensus.strength: near-universal among the run-path tools (crun, podman, microsandbox, smolvm, sbx, ERA)
consensus.implication_for_us: Make `run` the core ephemeral verb (--rm + trap + foreground-blocking, propagate workload exit code). Make persistence a --keep/--persist FLAG that stamps a label and skips --rm. This is the single most important verb-shape lesson.

consensus.pattern: Ephemeral-by-default for the one-shot path; persistent-by-default is the explicit anti-pattern that silently leaks containers/disk
consensus.strength: near-universal (every serious run-path tool is ephemeral; krunvm/sbx/claude-code-sandbox/arrakis are the cautionary leakers)
consensus.implication_for_us: Primary run = ephemeral via podman --rm. Cite sbx (silent disk growth) and claude-code-sandbox (orphans on CLI crash) as the exact failures our design prevents.

consensus.pattern: Multi-layer teardown: clean-exit auto-remove + signal/trap layer + crash backstop sweep (because --rm alone never covers SIGKILL/crash)
consensus.strength: common; independently converged on by gh-runner-krunvm and smolvm
consensus.implication_for_us: Lock the three-layer plan: --rm + bash trap on INT/TERM/EXIT calling podman rm -f -v + label-driven reap backstop at top of every invocation. Disarm the trap first to avoid re-entrant double-cleanup.

consensus.pattern: Engine/podman as the only source of truth; a separate state file/DB exists only to drift and force a reconcile reaper
consensus.strength: common (krunvm, microsandbox, smolvm, ERA, arrakis all carry a DB + reconciler; podman labels are the structured alternative)
consensus.implication_for_us: No state file, no DB. Podman labels (sandbox.managed-by/.created/.id/.worktree) are the single source of truth, giving reconciliation for free and deleting the entire drift bug class.

consensus.pattern: Select sandboxes by structured metadata/labels, never by name or prefix substring
consensus.strength: common (name-grep flagged unsafe in gh-runner-krunvm, claude-code-sandbox, ERA; podman --filter label= is the safe form)
consensus.implication_for_us: Select EXCLUSIVELY via podman --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us> (exclusive AND) for all list/reap. Strictly safer than every name-grep in the corpus.

consensus.pattern: Network-off / deny-by-default egress as the safe posture (serious tools converge; permissive ones flagged as footguns)
consensus.strength: common among serious tools (smolvm, podman none, sbx, microsandbox, several in the surveys)
consensus.implication_for_us: Make --network none the centralized default in the birth function; doctor asserts it is actually enforced. ERA's stored-but-unwired --network none is the cautionary no-op.

consensus.pattern: Conservative resource caps stamped at birth; unset/dev-sized defaults are dangerously generous
consensus.strength: common (krun, krunvm, era conservative; crun-vm all-host-CPUs, smolvm 4vCPU/8GiB, claude-code-sandbox no caps are the footguns)
consensus.implication_for_us: Always stamp small krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib (~1 vCPU / 256-1024 MiB), validated as integers in bash (krun fatals on malformed, silently ignores ram_mib<=128). Add rlimits (nofile/nproc) as a second layer.

consensus.pattern: Read-only mounts are the desired posture (though most tools default RW and bolt :ro on per-mount)
consensus.strength: common as aspiration; few default to it
consensus.implication_for_us: Invert the common default: mount read-only by default; the only RW mount is the tool-created git worktree, bound -v with :Z. Borrow sbx's path:ro suffix ergonomics for extra mounts.

consensus.pattern: Single shared security context (guest==VMM==user); containment comes from the HOST OS layer, and secrets must never enter the guest
consensus.strength: common (libkrun explicit; smolvm/agent-sandbox/sbx/gh-runner all forward creds rather than embed)
consensus.implication_for_us: Forward the host ssh-agent (bind $SSH_AUTH_SOCK + set the env var, keys never cross); hard-fail if no agent. Keep SELinux on, add no-new-privileges + cap-drop. Skip SEV/TDX (untrusted-host defense we don't need).

consensus.pattern: doctor / preconditions-as-verb-zero checking host prerequisites with actionable remediation before any run
consensus.strength: common (agent-sandbox names it; crun/libkrun/sbx imply it; gh-runner-krunvm and ERA are cautionary for lacking it)
consensus.implication_for_us: Make `doctor` verb-zero: check /dev/kvm accessible, crun +LIBKRUN feature string, crun-krun handler present, libkrun/libkrunfw loadable+ABI-matched. One remediation line per failure; distinct exit code; tie each check to the guarantee it underwrites.

consensus.pattern: Machine-readable output (--json/stable columns) separate from human tables; diagnostics/progress to stderr; never parse human tables
consensus.strength: common (microsandbox/sbx/podman; smolvm stderr discipline; ERA cited as gap for lacking it)
consensus.implication_for_us: Derive --json/stable columns straight from podman ps --filter label --format. Send logs/progress to stderr so stdout stays pipeable for the agent.

consensus.pattern: Stable distinct exit codes + verbatim forwarding of the workload's own exit code
consensus.strength: common (libkrun 125/126/127 model; krunvm collapsing all to 255 is the anti-pattern)
consensus.implication_for_us: Forward the workload exit code verbatim; reserve distinct codes for doctor/precondition-failure vs not-found vs launch-failure. Document the scheme in SKILL.md so the agent can branch.

consensus.pattern: Agent integration kept thin and EXTERNAL to the engine — skills/MCP wrap a stable CLI, never reach into engine internals
consensus.strength: common (microsandbox/arrakis external submodules; matches our no-plugin philosophy)
consensus.implication_for_us: The Claude SKILL.md wraps the script's stable verbs + documented exit codes only. Do NOT build an MCP server, REST/SDK, or reach into podman/krun internals.

consensus.pattern: Config = flags + env-var duality (flags win) with conservative defaults; no runtime state file
consensus.strength: common (gh-runner-krunvm ${VAR:-default}, smolvm/agent-sandbox/claude-code-sandbox file+flag merge; podman labels are the state)
consensus.implication_for_us: Adopt flags+env duality with ${VAR:-default}s, but reject any runtime state FILE; labels are the truth, queryable via --filter.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: Proves run=create+start in one shot and that krun is just crun + a flag (no krun-specific verbs); also the krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib annotation knobs with fatal-on-malformed and ram_mib<=128-silently-ignored semantics.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE: it is our underlying engine. Route caps through krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib annotations, validate integers in bash first.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: The explicit 'guest and VMM are ONE security context' statement plus capability-probe API (krun_has_feature/krun_check_nested_virt); also the fail-open defaults (TSI on, port_map(NULL) exposes all ports, root RW).
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE the security-context fact (justifies keys-never-in-guest) and the distinct-exit-code model; IGNORE forking it. Treat its fail-open defaults as the be-fail-closed warning.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: Cautionary trio: persistent-by-default with no --rm/trap/reaper, a confy state file + buildah store that drift, and DEAD declared-but-unimplemented flags (start --cpus/--mem, list -d, top-level -v).
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: IGNORE the architecture; USE the discipline rules: never ship a dead/no-op flag, and persistent-by-default is the leak we avoid.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: The daemonless engine whose labels/annotations ARE the state (queryable via ps --filter label=); plus concrete gotchas (--read-only-tmpfs defaults TRUE, --publish silently no-ops under --network none).
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE as our source of truth and enforcement layer; guard the documented gotchas (decide /tmp writability deliberately, never combine --publish with --network none).

per_repo_unique.unique_value: The pure-OCI-shim shape (no verbs, selected via --runtime crun-vm) and its flag-placement wart (flags must come AFTER the image, often need -- before them); --persistent can corrupt an image used by two VMs.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: IGNORE the shim shape; USE it as justification that our flag-preceded wrapper avoids the placement wart for a single-user tool.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: doctor that probes an in-IMAGE security dependency and warns guards 'fail open' if missing (check tied to guarantee); named the THE git-worktree-prune footgun; --lockdown profile vs escape-hatch hygiene.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE the doctor-ties-check-to-guarantee idea and the worktree-prune guard. IGNORE its SELinux label=disable default (anti-pattern on Fedora).

per_repo_unique.unique_value: Reaper-on-every-invocation (Once-guarded) reconciling SQLite DB vs live PIDs; plus a full egress DSL, TLS-intercept CA, --secret/--on-secret-violation, and per-exec rlimits.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE the reconcile-on-invocation PATTERN (but free via labels, no DB) and the rlimits idea. IGNORE/RESIST the net DSL, MITM CA, secret detector as adversary-model scope creep.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: Detached out-of-process reaper (_cleanup-ephemeral) returning the exit code immediately while teardown runs async, with PID+start_time identity verification and a strict is_safe_cache_path guard before any rm.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE the return-exit-code-now/reap-async split, the PID-reuse guard, and is_safe_cache_path verbatim for any host rm. IGNORE its forked libkrun and DNS-allowlist (off-mission).

per_repo_unique.unique_value: Two compounding lessons: (a) --network none was a complete NO-OP (stored, never wired to krunvm) — the headline fail-open; (b) base64 command-transport into the guest to dodge shell-quoting bugs.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE the base64 command-transport trick and the never-ship-metadata-only-isolation lesson. IGNORE its BoltDB and pluggable-backend abstraction.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: Platform-creep incarnate: root daemon + in-guest daemons + Go CLI + Python SDK + external MCP, plus destructive GLOBAL host mutation on boot (deletes all tap*, br0, flushes iptables).
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: STEAL NOTHING — steal the warning. It is the exact negative of our one-script/no-daemon design and the hard rule: never mutate host-global state, touch only labelled-as-ours resources.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: The complete three-layer teardown reference (--rm + EXIT/INT/TERM traps + prefix reaper), trap-disarm-first idiom, self-documenting usage() that greps its own option-comment lines, leveled log-to-fd helper; cautionary 30s-sleep concurrency mutex and missing KVM preflight.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE the trap-disarm-first idiom, self-documenting usage(), and leveled logging. IGNORE the runtime-abstraction seam; treat the 30s-sleep mutex as our graduation-to-Rust signal.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: Three named egress presets (Open/Balanced/Locked-Down) + policy allow|deny network DSL with deny>allow, host-proxy-enforced; documented footgun that rm of a clone-mode sandbox silently destroys unfetched commits; usermod -aG kvm first-run step.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE the path:ro suffix ergonomics, the named-tier vocabulary as positioning language, the usermod KVM precedent for doctor, and the silent-commit-loss as a hard rule. IGNORE the proxy/allowlist DSL (adversary model).

per_repo_unique.unique_value: Graduated stop<clean<purge cleanup ladder with confirmation/-y, but selection by NAME SUBSTRING for all list/stop/clean/purge and in-memory container Map lost on CLI crash (orphans only re-findable by name scan); a read-only config introspection verb.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE the read-only introspection idea (fold into doctor/--json). IGNORE the name-substring selection (our explicit anti-pattern) and the severity ladder (ephemeral default makes one reap enough).

per_repo_unique.unique_value: A field survey documenting that ephemeral-by-default (E2B/Sprites/Vercel/Modal), allowlisting (Matchlock), and separate-repo MCP servers are the field norm.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE as corroboration that ephemeral-by-default and external/thin agent integration are field consensus; no code to lift.

per_repo_unique.unique_value: Survey naming the canonical agent-facing access-tier vocabulary — Codex CLI's read-only / workspace-write / danger-full-access — and citing deny-by-default tools (Cleanroom/treebeard/Greywall/srt) with credential proxies.
per_repo_unique.use_or_ignore: USE the access-tier vocabulary IF we ever expose a single isolation-tier flag; otherwise corroborates deny-by-default + credential-proxy-not-keys-in-guest.

contested.question: Single `run` verb (collapse create+start) vs a two-phase create/start split?
contested.positions: microsandbox/smolvm/sbx split create (detached/persistent) from run (ephemeral); crun/podman/ERA treat run as create+start in one shot.
contested.our_call: Single `run` verb; persistence is an explicit --keep/--persist FLAG that stamps a label and skips --rm. The split exists only to serve persistent/detached lifecycles we default away from.

contested.question: Own thin verb set vs ride the engine surface as a pure OCI shim (no verbs)?
contested.positions: crun-vm/crun and agent-sandbox add zero verbs (selected by --runtime or flag presence); most others expose their own CLI.
contested.our_call: Own our own flag-preceded verbs above podman so we control UX, labeling, and cleanup — but still call podman run --runtime=krun underneath. The shim shape causes crun-vm's flag-placement wart.

contested.question: Egress control: network-policy DSL / allowlist proxy vs simple --network none?
contested.positions: sbx (named presets + proxy) and microsandbox (net-rule DSL + TLS-intercept) build adversary-grade egress control; smolvm uses a DNS allowlist; podman/krun default to no-NIC.
contested.our_call: --network none only (publish to localhost as the labelled exception). Our threat model is accident-not-adversary, so the proxy/allowlist DSL is explicit scope creep we reject; we borrow only the named-tier vocabulary as positioning.

contested.question: Separate runtime state file/DB vs engine labels as the single source of truth?
contested.positions: krunvm (confy), microsandbox (SQLite), smolvm (local DB), ERA (BoltDB), arrakis (in-memory maps) all keep state + a reconcile reaper; podman exposes labels as queryable state.
contested.our_call: Podman labels are the ONLY source of truth — no DB. This deletes the drift bug class that forced every DB-keeping tool to build a reconcile reaper; our reap becomes a free podman ps --filter sweep.

contested.question: Cleanup verb surface: graduated stop<clean<purge ladder vs a single reap backstop?
contested.positions: claude-code-sandbox/ERA expose a multi-step severity ladder (driven by persistent-by-default); ags/gh-runner rely on --rm + a single reaper.
contested.our_call: One `reap` backstop. The ladder is mostly a cautionary tale of persistent-by-default leaks; ephemeral --rm default means nothing survives the common case.

contested.question: Conservative vs dev-sized default resource caps?
contested.positions: era (1 vCPU/256MiB) and krunvm (2/1024) are conservative; smolvm (4 vCPU/8 GiB) is dev-sized; crun-vm (all host CPUs/2GiB unset) and claude-code-sandbox (no caps) are footguns.
contested.our_call: Conservative: ~1 vCPU / 256-1024 MiB stamped at birth via krun annotations, plus rlimits. Reject smolvm's dev-sizing and the never-leave-unset footgun.

contested.question: SELinux: keep MAC layer on (with :Z/:z relabel) vs disable it for convenience?
contested.positions: agent-sandbox defaults --security-opt=label=disable (SELinux off); podman/gh-runner use :Z/:z relabel (with the caveat :Z is private and can clobber shared dirs).
contested.our_call: Keep SELinux ON. Use :Z/:z only on the per-sandbox worktree we created (never a shared host dir); reject agent-sandbox's label=disable on Fedora.
